basic_details.job_title: WII Technical Assistant Recruitment 2026 - Apply Online for 50 Posts
basic_details.short_information: Every four years, India conducts the world's most comprehensive wildlife survey - the All India Tiger Estimation (AITE). The 2026 edition is now underway, and the Wildlife Institute of India (WII), the country's premier wildlife research institution based in Dehradun, is recruiting 50 Technical Assistants to support this landmark exercise in the field.

important_dates.expected joining: Shortly after selection (as per Offer Letter)

application_fee.details: The application fee structure differs by category. Payment must be completed before submitting the online application, and the payment receipt must be uploaded as part of the form. | Payment Methods Accepted: | Online/Electronic: NEFT / RTGS / Internet Banking / UPI (BHIM, PhonePe, Google Pay, Paytm) | Bank Details for NEFT/RTGS/Offline: | Account Name: RRP Cell Revolving / Director Wildlife Institute of India Bank: Union Bank of India Branch: Wildlife Institute of India, Chandrabani, Dehradun – 248 001 Account No.: 518502010059776 IFSC Code: UBIN0551856 | The application and processing fee is non-refundable once paid, regardless of whether the application is later rejected, not shortlisted, or the applicant withdraws.

qualification.details: The upper age limit for this position is 50 years. Age relaxation applies as per Government of India rules: SC/ST candidates receive 5 years of relaxation and OBC candidates receive 3 years, in accordance with DoPT OM No. 15012/2/2010-Estt.(D) dated 27.03.2012. | Candidates must hold one of the following: | Option A – Bachelor's Degree in Science In any of these disciplines from a recognized university: | Wildlife Science Zoology Ecology Environmental Science Geoinformatics Life Sciences Agriculture Forestry | Option B – 3-Year Diploma in Engineering From a recognized polytechnic or technical institution. | While not mandatory, the following will significantly strengthen a candidate's application: | Prior experience in wildlife research or biodiversity assessment Hands-on experience with ecological field surveys Camera trapping and telemetry work GIS and remote sensing applications Data management and analysis skills Experience preparing technical reports for forest departments or research institutions

salary_details.details: Selected Technical Assistants will receive a monthly consolidated emolument of ₹27,000 plus HRA (House Rent Allowance) as applicable. This is a project-based contractual position. | The initial project duration is one year, with a likelihood of extension depending on project requirements and satisfactory performance. Candidates should note that these are purely temporary, project-specific positions and will stand abolished upon completion of the AITE 2026 project. No claim for regular appointment at WII will arise from this engagement. | In-Hand Salary Calculator 2026

selection_process: Understanding the full selection pipeline helps you prepare at each stage.

Stage 1 – Application Screening All applications are reviewed for completeness and basic eligibility. Incomplete applications or those missing required documents/fee receipts will be rejected at this stage.

Stage 2 – Merit-Based Shortlisting Eligible applications are scored on the 50-point criteria (25 marks for academic percentage + 25 marks for field/research relevance). Only the top 10 candidates per position will be called for the interview.
Stage 3 – Online Interview A duly constituted Selection Committee at WII conducts online interviews for shortlisted candidates. Candidates must arrange their own internet access for the interview.

Stage 4 – Announcement of Results The list of shortlisted candidates for interview and the final selection results will be published in the "Latest News & Announcements" section at https://wii.gov.in/news. No individual intimation will be sent separately.
Stage 5 – Joining Selected candidates must join the project position immediately within the period specified in the Offer Letter. Delay in joining may result in the offer being cancelled.
Note: WII reserves the right to consider a candidate for a lower-grade position if found not suitable for the post applied
as per the Interview Committee's recommendation.
how_to_apply: WII accepts online applications only. No printed or posted application will be considered under any circumstances. Here is the complete process:
Step 1 – Gather Your Documents First Before opening the application form, scan and keep the following ready in digital format:
Educational qualification certificates and all marksheets
Work experience certificates (mentioning exact start and end dates, from employer/guide/supervisor)
Publications or conference paper details (if any)
Step 2 – Pay the Application Fee Complete payment via UPI, NEFT, RTGS, or internet banking to the account details provided above. Save the payment receipt/screenshot for upload.

Step 3 – Fill the Online Application Form Visit https://wiirp.com/application.php and complete the application. Enter all educational details exactly as they appear on your marksheets - no approximations or corrections will be accepted after submission.

Step 4 – Upload All Supporting Documents Attach all scanned copies of certificates, marksheets, experience letters, category certificate, fee receipt, photo, and signature. Incomplete applications missing any of these will be rejected without any notice.
Step 5 – Submit Before the Deadline The portal closes on June 30, 2026. Applications submitted after this date will not be entertained.

faq.question: Q1. What is the WII Technical Assistant Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: It is a recruitment by the Wildlife Institute of India (WII) for 50 Technical Assistant positions under the All India Tiger Estimation (AITE 2026) project. The positions are offered on a 1-year contractual basis with a monthly emolument of ₹27,000 plus HRA. Applications are accepted only through the online portal at https://wiirp.com/application.php.

faq.question: Q2. What is the last date to apply for WII Technical Assistant 2026?
faq.answer: The online application portal closes on June 30, 2026. No applications will be accepted after this date, and no offline or printed applications will be considered.

faq.question: Q3. What is the salary for WII Technical Assistant under AITE 2026?
faq.answer: Selected candidates will receive ₹27,000 per month plus HRA (House Rent Allowance) as applicable. This is a fixed emolument for the duration of the 1-year project, which is likely to be extended.

faq.question: Q4. What educational qualification is needed for WII Technical Assistant 2026?
faq.answer: Candidates must hold a Bachelor's degree in science in disciplines such as Wildlife Science, Zoology, Ecology, Environmental Science, Geoinformatics, Life Sciences, Agriculture, or Forestry from a recognized university. Alternatively, a 3-year Diploma in Engineering is also accepted.

faq.question: Q5. Is there an age limit for this recruitment?
faq.answer: The upper age limit is 50 years. SC/ST candidates are eligible for 5 years of age relaxation, and OBC candidates for 3 years, as per Government of India DoPT guidelines.

faq.question: Q6. What is the application fee for WII AITE 2026 Technical Assistant?
faq.answer: General category candidates must pay ₹500 as the application fee. Candidates belonging to SC, ST, OBC, EWS, or Persons with Disability (PwD) categories must pay ₹100 as a processing fee. The fee is non-refundable in all cases.

faq.question: Q7. How will candidates be shortlisted for the interview?
faq.answer: Shortlisting is based on a 50-point scoring system: 25 marks for the percentage of marks in the Bachelor's degree, and 25 marks for the relevance of the candidate's specialization to the AITE project (evidenced through internship, volunteering, or prior work experience). Only the top 10 scorers per position will be called for an online interview.

faq.question: Q8. What kind of work will a Technical Assistant do under AITE 2026?
faq.answer: Technical Assistants will conduct camera trap surveys, line transect surveys, sign surveys, and habitat assessments in the field. On the data processing side, they will geo-tag and process camera trap photographs using CaTRAT software, segregate images by species, and identify individual tigers and leopards using Extract Compare software and manual identification methods.

faq.question: Q9. Can I apply for more than one position at WII?
faq.answer: Yes, candidates can apply for a maximum of two advertised positions if they meet the eligibility for each. However, each position requires a separate online application form with its own complete set of documents and a separate fee payment.

faq.question: Q10. Where will the results be published?
faq.answer: Shortlisted candidates for interview and final selection results will be published in the "Latest News & Announcements" section of WII's official website at https://wii.gov.in/news. Candidates are advised to check this page regularly.

title: WII Technical Assistant Recruitment 2026 - Apply Online for 50 Posts
